plugin_desc: Huawei-specific SNMP collection for switches. In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects Huawei private OIDs (HUAWEI-ENTITY-EXTENT-MIB) for device health: CPU utilization, memory utilization, temperature sensors and fan status. Huawei reports memory as a utilization percentage directly and does not expose a power-supply state OID in this template.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the Huawei device control plane (HUAWEI-ENTITY-EXTENT-MIB hwEntityCpuUsage), normalized to a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the Huawei device (HUAWEI-ENTITY-EXTENT-MIB hwEntityMemUsage), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the temperature reading of each Huawei hardware sensor (HUAWEI-ENTITY-EXTENT-MIB hwEntityTemperature), in degrees Celsius. Abnormally high temperatures may indicate fan failure, blocked airflow, or excessive ambient heat, and can trigger thermal protection or hardware damage if left unaddressed.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the operational state of each Huawei cooling fan (HUAWEI-ENTITY-EXTENT-MIB hwEntityFanState). Huawei reports a binary state where 1 means normal and 2 means abnormal; any value other than normal indicates a cooling fault that can quickly lead to overheating and should be investigated immediately.
